input.instruction: A weakener fired on a credibility evidence package. Using ONLY the supplied pattern definition and measures, explain what the flag means, why it fired, and what the engineer should do. Choose ONE action_class from the §5B vocabulary, report a verbalized confidence, and escalate if uncertain. Stay within measure-don't-judge: report on the evidence package, never on whether the model is correct.

fired_pattern.definition: Evaluation regime crosses a physical regime boundary the training set did not cover.

case_context.cou: surrogate predicts heat flux at Mach 6.5 for a TPS sizing decision

answer_key.gold_mechanism: An 8% numeric Mach increase looks small, but it crosses the real-gas dissociation threshold the training set never sampled, so the COU sits in a physically distinct regime — applicability is not established despite the small numeric margin.

gold_action.posture_basis: standard-derived (ASME V&V 40: credibility bounded to the validated domain)

forbidden_claims: asserts the heat-flux prediction is wrong (a verdict the regime gap does not support)
treats the small numeric Mach margin as evidence of applicability across the regime boundary

hardness.obvious_signal: Mach number 6.5 is only 8% above the training maximum of 6.0 — barely outside
hardness.conflicting_signal: the 8% Mach increase crosses the real-gas dissociation threshold (~Mach 6.2); the training set is entirely below it, so the COU is in a physically distinct regime the surrogate never saw, not a small extrapolation

label_provenance.external_grounding: ASME V&V 40 applicability + hypersonics: a small numeric parameter change that crosses a physical regime boundary (real-gas effects) is a regime extrapolation, not interpolation.

by_field.gold_action.selected_class: provisional-self-adjudicated (row-builder; needs independent expert/solver adjudication before disposition-gate use)
